title: Ceramic hob cleaning paste
subtitle: A bicarbonate and citric acid paste for burnt-on residue on ceramic hobs
excerpt: Bicarbonate of soda provides mild abrasion and alkaline cleaning action. Citric acid dissolves mineral deposits and loosens burnt-on food. Used together in sequence (not combined), they tackle the two main types of residue on a ceramic hob without scratching the surface.
sourceNotes: Ceramic hob cleaning from The Complete Book of Clean, Lim (2019). Citric acid and bicarbonate chemistry from DIY Natural (diynatural.com, May 2026).

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 cleaning treatment for a ceramic hob
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make fresh for each cleaning session. The citric acid solution and the bicarbonate paste are applied in sequence and work best fresh.
recipe.temperatureNote: Use only on a cold hob. Never apply cleaning products to a hot ceramic surface: thermal shock can crack the glass.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use immediately. Prepared bicarbonate paste will dry out within hours. Citric acid solution keeps in a spray bottle for up to 1 week. Store any remaining citric acid solution in a labelled spray bottle.

recipeTools.notes: Or any mixing bowl for the bicarbonate paste

glossaryTerms.definition: Ceramic and induction hob surfaces are smooth glass-ceramic. They scratch easily with abrasive pads or steel wool. Only use soft cloths, silicone scrapers, or paste-based cleaners. Never use bicarbonate of soda dry: always make it into a paste first to reduce the abrasive effect. Never clean a hot hob.

content.text: Ceramic hob cleaning works in two stages because the two types of residue need different chemistry. Mineral deposits (limescale, hard water rings) respond to acid: citric acid solution. Burnt-on food and grease respond to alkali and abrasion: bicarbonate paste. Applying them in sequence rather than combined gives better results than either alone.
Always clean a cold hob: never apply water or cleaning products to a hot surface. Follow
guidelines throughout. For very heavy burnt-on residue, use a ceramic hob scraper (a purpose-made razor tool) at 30 degrees to the surface before applying the paste.

items.prepNote: Combined with a small amount of water to make a paste; for burnt-on food and grease
Dissolved in 300 ml water as a spray; for mineral deposits and hard water marks
Added to the bicarbonate paste for surfactant action

content.text: Confirm the hob is cold before starting. Dissolve the citric acid in 300 ml of water in a spray bottle. Set aside.

Make the bicarbonate paste: combine bicarbonate of soda and castile liquid soap in a bowl, adding water a teaspoon at a time until a thick paste forms. Apply the paste to the hob surface with a damp cloth. Leave for 10 minutes. Scrub gently with a soft cloth or silicone scraper. Wipe away the paste with a damp cloth.
Spray the citric acid solution over the hob. Leave for 5 minutes to dissolve any remaining mineral deposits. Wipe away with a clean damp cloth. Dry with a soft cloth to prevent watermarks.
Make the bicarbonate paste fresh each time. Remaining citric acid solution keeps in a labelled spray bottle for up to 1 week. Keep both away from children.
